class.name: Cubemap
class.brief_description: A single texture resource which consists of 6 faces. Each face is an [Image].
class.description: A [TextureLayered] of type [code]enum TextureLayeredType.TEXTURE_LAYERED_CUBEMAP[/code] which uses exactly 6 layers. Used in reflection (see [ReflectionProbe]), lighting and shadowing systems. A [Cubemap] holds exactly 6 [Image]s. One image in each layer. The 6 images are the 6 faces of the cube. They will be handled as X+, X-, Y+, Y-, Z+, Z-. See [enum VisualServer.CubeMapLayer]. Godot does not use the [Cubemap] to create skyboxes (see [PanoramaSky]), but rather uses its image data to influence the reflections ([ReflectionProbe]), lighting ([GIProbe], more on GI [url=https://godotengine.org/article/godot-3-0-released#pbr-gi]here[/url] ) and shadows (see [code]struct ShadowCubemap[/code] in [code]RasterizerSceneRD[/code]) in the scene via the [Sky]. [TextureLayered]'s class reference explains how a [Cubemap] operates and how it has to be handled. It is based on the class [Texture] (not [Texture2D]) and can only used as such. Its internal resource type, as well as recognized file extension, is [code].cube[/code]. The [Cubemap] is one cubemap level with an array of exactly 6 [Image]s. Mipmaps are optional. In Godot, the [Cubemap] can be used for the [Sky]. Your [ProjectSettings] value for "rendering/quality/reflections/texture_array_reflections" is reflected in [code]RasterizerSceneRD[/code]s private member [code]sky_use_cubemap_array[/code]. If this member is set to [code]false[/code], the [Sky] will use a [Cubemap]. Otherwise, the [CubemapArray] logic will be used. The Sky's [code]radiance[/code] and the [code]RasterizerEffectsRD[/code] [code]roughness[/code] will be a [code]CubemapRoughness[/code] with [code]CUBEMAP_ROUGHNESS_SOURCE_CUBEMAP[/code] as its [code]CubemapRoughnessSource[/code]. Your [ReflectionProbe]s will be affected by the [Cubemap] in your [Sky]. This can be turned off for each [ReflectionProbe], if its [code]Interior[/code] property is enabled. [Cubemap] has [code]enum RenderingDevice.TextureType[/code] [code]TEXTURE_TYPE_CUBE[/code]. Currently, [Cubemap]s cannot be created automatically. You can create them externally with a 3rd party tool or using the basisu commandline tool (see file [code]thirdparty/basis_universal/basisu_tool.cpp[/code] regarding [code]-tex_type cubemap[/code]). It can also be created manually from several [Image]s via C++ code. In case you want to use a cubemap for your [Sky] (and not just some of its data), see [PanoramaSky]'s class documentation on how to convert your cubemap to a panorama.
